falsification_conditions.condition: The S&P 500 closes above its 200-day moving average by 3% or more
falsification_conditions.what_it_would_mean: This would indicate a strong bullish trend contradicting the 'hold' signal, suggesting the pattern is not a bottom but a continuation of an uptrend.

falsification_conditions.condition: Volume on the NASDAQ exceeds 200% of its 30-day average volume for 3 consecutive days
falsification_conditions.what_it_would_mean: Such a surge in volume would signal extreme market participation, invalidating the 'unknown' verdict by confirming a clear directional move (likely bullish) contrary to the hold signal.

falsification_conditions.condition: The CBOE VIX spikes above 35 and remains there for 5 consecutive trading days
falsification_conditions.what_it_would_mean: A sustained spike in volatility would indicate a market breakdown or panic, directly contradicting the 'hold' signal and suggesting the pattern is not a bottom but a pre-blowoff phase.

doctrine: If you can't list what would prove your call wrong, you don't have a real call.
